visualCorrection.issue: The P1 screenshot harness opened /admin/compliance/audit without the platform-admin role query, so it captured the current-role fallback instead of the compliance audit state.
visualCorrection.fix: client/e2e/durable-sagas-compliance.spec.ts now opens /admin/compliance/audit?role=platform-admin, verifies the platform-admin branch, clicks Search, and waits for audit records before capturing screenshots.
visualCorrection.verification: FLOW-19 Playwright P1 suite reran after the fix and passed 18/18.

commands.command: Push-Location client; npx playwright test durable-sagas-compliance.spec.ts --reporter=list; Pop-Location

notes: Shows Compliance Audit Records with three audit records and action controls.
No access-denied fallback remains after the harness correction.
Domain fields visible: saga id, event type, legal hold, written date, expiry date, status.
Platform-admin sees the executable durable saga dashboard.
State badges are readable and not color-only.
Tenant-user sees a plain-language summary with no saga internals or write controls.
Mobile layout is readable without overlap.
Platform-support sees read-only context and an escalation path.
Write controls are disabled and visually distinct.

followUps: Real GitHub repo screenshots remain required during the fork cascade phases.
Hebrew RTL and French spot checks are outside this FLOW-19 portability phase and are not required for this auth/cascade certification close-out.
